frameworks.name: Bayesian causal structure learning
frameworks.one_line: Beliefs about causal structure between contexts and cues are updated via Bayesian inference

frameworks.name: associative learning
frameworks.one_line: Stimulus-stimulus associations learned under a given causal structure

frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis
frameworks.one_line: Used to probe distributed encoding of probability distributions over structures

core_question: What are the neural substrates that arbitrate between abstract causal structures versus those that learn stimulus associations under a given structure?
key_assumptions: Beliefs about causal structure constrain which stimuli enter into association
Bayesian updating governs acquisition of structural beliefs from experience
Structure arbitration and association learning are computationally distinct
manipulations: Cue-outcome contingencies consistent with different latent causal structures
Context manipulations modulating presumed causal relationships
key_findings: Structure learning signals encoded in rostrolateral prefrontal cortex and angular gyrus
Associative learning signals are anatomically distinct from structure learning correlates
Within-subject encoding variability predicts behavioral performance variability
Inferior frontal gyrus may encode the full posterior over causal structures
interpretations: Distinct neural systems support causal-structure arbitration versus association learning
Some regions represent the full distribution over candidate causal structures, not just a point estimate
